description:
Want the best guidance on buying health (or term) insurance from IRDAI-certified experts? Book a FREE call with Ditto today - https://ditto.sh/zpjc2o

#acko #ackohealth #healthinsurancepolicy 

Is Acko Health Insurance actually worth buying? In today’s video, we look into Acko Health, specifically their Platinum Plan, and break down whether it’s a solid choice or just good on paper.

If you're currently searching for the best health insurance plan in India and confused between traditional players like HDFC Ergo, ICICI Lombard, Star Health, or the newer ones like Acko Health Insurance, then this video is for you. We’ll cover everything from Acko Health Insurance features, pricing, pros and cons, and who should ideally consider going for it.

Now coming to Acko Health Insurance. Acko is a tech-first insurance company that’s relatively new but backed by big names like Amazon, Accel, and Catamaran. It promises a direct-to-customer experience, zero intermediaries, paperless policy issuance, and quick digital claims. Sounds futuristic, right?

In this video, we’ve done a complete Acko Health Insurance review—comparing their plans, benefits, claims data, and pricing. The most popular product, Acko Platinum Plan, claims to offer zero waiting period, zero room rent limit, no copay, and full bill coverage. On paper, the Acko Health Platinum Plan is one of the most attractive options in the market, especially for those looking for large sum insured options at a low premium.

We discuss Acko Health’s claim settlement ratio, which stands at an impressive average of 96.43 percent over the last 3 years, much higher than the industry average. Acko Health Insurance also has over 10,000 network hospitals across India, so cashless claims are pretty accessible.

But we also cover the caveats. Despite advertising zero waiting periods, Acko Health Insurance may still apply up to 3 years of waiting for pre-existing conditions depending on the underwriting. Also, Acko Health Insurance is still a relatively new player in health insurance, which means it doesn’t yet have the decades-long track record of other major insurers. So if you’re someone who’s uncomfortable managing claims on your own or prefers an agent-driven service, that’s something to consider.

We also talk about Acko Health Insurance pricing. For a 30-year-old individual in Delhi, Acko Platinum Plan costs just around Rs. 11,000 annually for a 1 crore cover. For a couple, it’s Rs. 19,000, which is significantly cheaper than other comparable plans like ICICI Lombard Elevate or HDFC Ergo. This is because Acko Health Insurance follows a zero-agent model, which brings down the operational cost, and in turn, the premiums.

But we also raise important questions. Is unlimited coverage necessary when most treatments don’t even cross 1 crore? Are you okay dealing directly with the insurer in case of a complex claim?

So if you are young, healthy, digitally savvy, and looking for a plan that gives maximum features at a minimum cost, Acko Health Insurance Platinum Plan might be a great pick. But if you have pre-existing conditions or need support during claims, you might want to consider other options or make sure you’re okay with how Acko Health works.

Our verdict? Acko Health Insurance is one of the most affordable, digitally enabled health plans today. But it’s not one-size-fits-all. That’s why personalisation matters.

If you’re confused between Acko Health Insurance and other plans, and want honest, spam-free advice, then book a free call with Ditto. Our advisors will help you pick the right policy, explain the fine print, and stay with you during claims too. https://ditto.sh/zpjc2o

That’s it for this video. Don’t forget to like, comment, and subscribe if you want more such unbiased reviews on health and term insurance plans.

Connect with Us:

Happy Insurance Hunting!

comments.author: @amanlamba8436

comments.text:
I was very excited when I purchased the ACKO Platinum Health Policy, which promised ₹1 crore coverage with no waiting period for any treatment. Based on the commitments mentioned in their policy documents, the policy appeared highly attractive. Unfortunately, my experience during the actual claim process was completely different.
My first cashless claim was rejected by ACKO, stating that the cataract condition had existed before the policy start date and was not disclosed at the time of purchasing the policy. In reality, we were not aware of any such condition when the policy was bought. The claim was made when the policy was only four months old.
ACKO promotes itself as a customer-friendly and technology-driven insurance company that aims to eliminate common gaps in the health insurance industry. However, despite their strong marketing claims, their processes during policy issuance and claim assessment are poorly defined and executed. The reliance on technology does not compensate for the lack of transparency and fairness during claim evaluation.
I strongly advise others to be cautious and not fall for ACKO’s misleading marketing strategies. While they highlight a ₹1 crore sum assured, such coverage becomes meaningless when even genuine cashless claims are rejected. My experience has been disappointing and does not align with the commitments presented at the time of policy purchase.
comments.author: @thoratpiyush

comments.text: Never go with ACKO. They are totally unprofessional Bunch.  They will not do anything.
comments.author: @subhobratamitra8387

comments.text:
I've an Acko platinum health plan for my mother and father. My father (aged 70) got admitted in emergency, Apollo Sage Hospital, Bhopal for femur (IT) fracture on 27.10.2025 and got discharged on 12.11.2025. My first hand unbiased experience with Acko is as under:
1. Their tpa FHPL is very responsive. 
2. You just have to whatsapp or mail your policy e-card and aadhar card to the hospital tpa desk and that's it. No other documents required.
3. Once admitted and initial approval is obtained from fhpl, everything else is handled directly by Acko (and not fhpl) - this is a brilliant thing because we can directly speak with Acko regarding our case and we don't need to rush to hospital tpa desk or fhpl. 
4. Since Acko is a tech based zero physical presence company, they've mastered the art of giving regular updates on WhatsApp, email and sms. Claim initiated, claim under process, additional documents requested (in your email you'll get the details of documents/ information that Acko has requested from the hospital), claim approved - this is important because patient's attendant can remain relaxed that the company is actively working on their claim and not be in dark.
5. Now the bestest part, even if doctor's letter for enhancement, etc has some problem or hospital doesn't respond on additional documents request, Acko will directly call you to explain everything, what comes under policy exclusion, etc and even offer solutions as to how the claim can be positively handled - which company does this??!! Insurance companies mostly seek quick opportunities to reject your claim or to curtail it at best, but I'm amazed and shocked at the way Acko pushed me to get my claim approved. Even if hospital tpa desk doesn't respond, you can yourself send required documents by contacting your doctor and just mail them directly to Acko and that's it.
6. Based on doctor's advice, hospital TPA desk requested for enhancement of amount and extension of admission from Acko. Same was granted within 2-3 hours. I repeat , no delays at all. Trust me guyz, you'll have to put no extra effort at anything.
7. I've done extensive research before buying health policy. Social media space is filled with hate and criticism of Acko which is not true at all. Acko doesn't have affiliate connections with policybazaar, etc and no agents, no offices. That's why they are looked at with envy. Their premiums are low and policy wordings are crystal clear. App is fantastic and workflow is brilliant right from payment to policy purchase to claim. 
8. No matter what the noise is all around, guyz please do your own research and go for your gut feeling while buying health insurance. Sponsored reviews, big ad campaigns, aggresive call marketing, etc don't work in a serious issue like heath insurance. The simple, the better and Acko definitely has mastered this.
9. Last but not the least, Acko doesn't call-bomb you for marketing or for this policy, that policy,etc. 1 or 2 calls and that's enough. Their health policies don't have 1000 different riders and add-ons. Just one compact product and that's it. 
10. Theirs doctors team is beyond humane!! They're fabulous is all sense possible in showing empathy, care, concern and cooperation in every possible way.
11. I could write endlessly but remember, if you've not hidden anything while buying policy and disclosed everything clearly, not even a penny will be deducted from final amount. In my case, 5.78 L was the final amount and the approved amount was 5.78 L !!! They even paid pre-hospitalization ambulance charge and post hospitalization/ follow up treatment cost with lightening speed as promised.
12. Thank you Acko, I'm your policyholder for life :) avoid noise guyz, hopefully my review will help you all in giving confidence and trust in the company.

ACKO Platinum requires a detailed health check-up before policy issuance. Based on that, the company evaluates whether a waiting period should be applied. If any PED is disclosed or detected at the time of application, depending on its severity, the insurer decides the applicable waiting period.

Premium increases are common and usually happen due to age, inflation, or company-wide revisions. If you’re not satisfied with the plan or premium, you can get in touch with our advisors — they’ll assess your family’s profile and suggest suitable alternatives so you can decide whether to continue or switch.

comments.text: Will Acko increase premiums drastically in future ?
comments.author: @roysocanalyst9172

comments.text:
It’s not possible to say with certainty whether Acko will drastically increase premiums in the future. However, premium revisions are a normal part of the insurance industry and are seen across all insurers from time to time. The extent of any future premium hike typically depends on multiple factors, such as:

- Current product pricing strategy – If the initial premiums are aggressively low to attract customers, adjustments may be likely over time.

- Claims experience – A higher number of claims than anticipated can lead insurers to revise premiums to maintain profitability.

- Underwriting quality – If the insurer has been lenient in accepting high-risk cases, it could affect their claims ratio, influencing future pricing.

- Features and benefits offered – Plans with broader coverage or additional features may warrant higher premiums later, especially if those features increase claim payouts.

While Acko’s current premiums are attractive, being a relatively new player, there’s still limited long-term data on how they manage pricing changes. It's wise to keep these factors in mind when evaluating the sustainability of their rates.
comments.author: @DittoInsuranceHindi
